title: Scenario applicability assessment result (row mode)
description: Output of `/ir:onboard-agent assess --row <scenario>`. Maps every adapter in .specs/agent-scenarios-coverage.json -> agents[] to a candidate applicability verdict for one scenario, with citations. Consumed by the maintainer to update the coverage matrix row-by-row; never auto-applied. Schema validates structural shape only — cross-referencing against canonical adapter IDs is done at runtime by run-batch.sh.

schema_version.description: Bump when the schema shape changes.

scenario_version.description: Optional. Git commit SHA of .specs/agent-scenarios.md at assessment time. Helps a re-runner notice when the prose moved.

survey_tool_version.description: Git short SHA of the skill at run time. Optional; useful when comparing two assessments of the same scenario.

source_catalog.description: Path of the scenario catalog the assessment ran against (default `.specs/agent-scenarios.md`).

adapters.description: Keyed by adapter ID (lowercase slug from .specs/agent-scenarios-coverage.json -> agents[].id). Not every adapter has to appear — adapters whose capabilities[] don't include the scenario's required features may be omitted, with the implicit verdict 'n/a'. run-batch.sh checks that every key resolves to a known adapter ID.

agent_supports.description: Mirrors the `agent_supports` axis of .specs/agent-scenarios-coverage.json so the row can be diffed against the committed matrix cell-for-cell.

confidence.description: 0..1. Low confidence (<0.5) flags cells the maintainer should hand-verify. `unknown` verdicts typically carry low confidence.

sources.description: Citations the verdict was derived from. At least one is required — the assessment must show its work.

notes.description: Free-form. Names the gotcha, the issue number, or the unmet condition.

prerequisites_hint.description: If `agent_supports` is gated on maintainer-only setup (paid plan, signing cert, API key), a one-line description that the maintainer can copy into `replaydata/agents/<agent>/prerequisites.md`.

kind.description: Coarse-grained citation type. `file` is a local path; everything else is a URL or identifier.

ref.description: URL or repo-relative file path. For `issue`, use the form `owner/repo#123` or a full URL.

excerpt.description: Optional verbatim quote (<=300 chars) so the maintainer can sanity-check the claim without re-fetching.
